revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Bureau of Religions]][[ja:宗教局]]


{{Infobox organization
| formation = April 1900
| predecessor = [[Bureau of Shrines and Temples]]
| successor = 
| dissolved = November 1, 1942
| parent_organization = {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Home Ministry|1=Home Ministry}} (1900-1913), {{ill|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|en|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|qid=Q11080185|lt=Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|1=Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture}} (1913-1942)
}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Bureau of Religions'''|宗教局|Shūkyō kyoku|qq=}} is one of the {{ill|Internal departments of Imperial Japan|lt=Internal departments|ja|内部部局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Internal departments of Imperial Japan|1=Internal Subdivision|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11394606}} of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of the Interior|1=Home Ministry}} during the pre-{{ill|World War I|en|World War I|qid=Q361|lt=World War I|1=World War I}} period and later the {{ill|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|en|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|qid=Q11080185|lt=Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|1=Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture}} in Japan.
It was split off from the [[Bureau of Shrines and Temples]] in 1900.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html#shaji_kyoku |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Mullins |first1=Mark R. |last2=Thomas |first2=Jolyon Baraka |last3=McMullen |first3=Matthew D. |date=2022 |title=Religion, Politics, and the Law in Postwar Japan |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/48696755 |journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies |volume=49 |issue=1 |pages=118 |doi=10.18874/jjrs.49.1.2022.115-146 |jstor=48696755 |s2cid=253606405 |issn=0304-1042|doi-access=free |hdl=2292/62450 |hdl-access=free |qq=}}</ref>
== Outline ==
Initially, religious administration was under the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Shrines and Temples Bureau|Bureau of Shrines and Temples]] of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of the Interior|1=Home Ministry}}, but in April 1900, it was separated into the [[Bureau of Shrines]] and the Bureau of Religion.<ref name="asia1">{{Jacar|A03020457500|御署名原本・明治三十三年・勅令第百六十三号・内務省官制中改正追加|qq=}}、明治33rd year 4月26日。</ref> The first and second divisions were set up with jurisdictions over [[Shinto]], {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|qid=Q736311|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism in Japan}}, and other religious affairs, as well as {{ill|Bhikkhu|en|Bhikkhu|qid=Q854997|lt=monks'|1=Bhikkhu}} and {{ill|Clergy|en|Clergy|qid=Q177826|lt=teachers'|1=Clergy}} affairs.{{Sfn|秦郁彦 |1981|page=738|qq=}}
On June 13, 1913, the Bureau of Religion was transferred from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Home Ministry|1=Home Ministry}} to the {{ill|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|en|Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|qid=Q11080185|lt=Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture|1=Ministry of Education, Science, Sports and Culture}}.{{Sfn|秦郁彦 |1981|page=738|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web |script-title=ja:文化庁月報 平成25th year 9月号(No.540) |url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/pr/publish/bunkachou_geppou/2013_09/special_03/special_03.html |access-date=2021-04-21 |publisher=文化庁|qq=}}</ref> It was in charge of denominations, sects, associations, priests, teachers, and other matters related to religion, such as Buddhist temples and the preservation of old shrines and temples.<ref name="asia2">{{Jacar|A03020971300|御署名原本・大正二年・勅令第百七十三号・文部省官制中改正|qq=}}、大正2nd year 6月13日。</ref><ref name="kanpo1">[{{NDLDC|2952363|/1/8|format=url|qq=}} 『官報』第265号「彙報」大正2nd year 6月18日。] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} {{NDLPID|2952363|qq=}}</ref>
On December 22, 1924, the First Section was renamed the Religious Affairs Section and the Second Section was renamed the Ancient Shrine and Temple Preservation Section.<ref name="kanpo2">[{{NDLDC|2955852|/1/5|format=url|qq=}} 『官報』第3704号「彙報」大正13rd year 12月25日。] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} {{NDLPID|2955852|qq=}}</ref> On December 1, 1928, the Division for the Preservation of Ancient Temples and Shrines was renamed the Preservation Division.<ref name="kanpo3">[{{NDLDC|2957046|/1/3|format=url|qq=}} 『官報』第581号「彙報」昭和3rd year 12月4日。] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} {{NDLPID|2957046|qq=}}</ref> In 1942 it was abolished and went through a few successor institutions until complete abolition after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}.{{Sfn|秦郁彦 |1981|page=747|qq=}}
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Citation |last=[[秦郁彦]] |title=戦前期日本官僚制の制度・組織・人事 |date=1981 |url= |volume= |pages= |series= |edition= |publisher=東京大学出版会 |doi= |isbn= |id= |author-link= |translator-last=|qq=}}
* {{Citation |last=[[秦郁彦]] |title=日本官僚制総合事典 : 1868-2000 |date=2001 |url= |volume= |pages= |series= |edition= |publisher=東京大学出版会 |doi= |isbn=4130301217 |id= |author-link= |translator-last=|qq=}}
{{State Shinto|qq=}}
<noinclude></noinclude><noinclude></noinclude>
